w14.title: Electrical kitchen appliances & lighting (3)
w14.topic: Energy use of blender, coffee maker and LED lamps; comparing motor, heating and lighting loads.
w14.lo1: Identify the energy conversion in motor, heating and lighting loads.
w14.lo2: Calculate energy consumption and cost for blender, coffee maker and LED lamps.
w14.lo3: Compare the energy efficiency of different lamp wattages.
w14.lo4: Distinguish power factor for resistive, motor and electronic loads.

theory.title: Theory — Conservation of Energy & Electricity
theory.intro: The Law of Conservation of Energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another.
theory.acIntro: In AC circuits voltage and current may not be in phase, therefore:
theory.acNote: The power factor (cos φ) indicates efficiency: cos φ = 1 → purely resistive (kettle, toaster); cos φ < 1 → losses exist (motors, electronics).

blender.name: 1 — Blender
blender.howBody: A blender uses an electric motor to rotate the blades. Electrical energy is converted into mechanical energy; the motor rotates the blades and the food is mixed.
blender.phys2: Motor rotates the blades at high speed
blender.phys3: Food is cut and mixed by blade motion
blender.calcBody: Power = 0.5 kW. Time = 5 min. Using E = P × t: E = 0.5 × (5/60) ≈ 0.04 kWh. At €0.30/kWh that is ≈ €0.01.

coffee.name: 2 — Coffee Maker
coffee.howBody: A coffee maker heats water and brews coffee. Electrical energy is converted directly into heat; the heated water flows through the coffee grounds and produces coffee.
coffee.phys2: Water is heated and flows through ground coffee
coffee.calcBody: Power = 1 kW. Time = 10 min. Using E = P × t: E = 1 × (10/60) ≈ 0.166 kWh. At €0.30/kWh that is ≈ €0.05.

led.name: 3 — LED Lamps
led.howBody: LED (Light Emitting Diode) lamps convert electrical energy into light very efficiently. Most of the input energy becomes visible light, with very little waste heat.
led.phys4: Small power factor cos φ < 1 due to driver electronics
led.calcBody: Power = 0.005 kW. Time = 60 min. Using E = P × t: E = 0.005 × (60/60) ≈ 0.005 kWh. At €0.30/kWh that is ≈ €0.0015.
led.compareTitle: Energy comparison — 5 W vs. 10 W LED

quiz.title: Quiz — multiple choice (15 questions)
quiz.intro: Choose the correct answer for each question. Q1–5: blender. Q6–10: coffee maker. Q11–15: LED lamps.
